diary: Rain did that soft, gray thing all morning—started before I even got out of bed in Newcastle, so the kitchen looked hazy through the window. I made coffee, did a quick reset, and grabbed a simple breakfast to-go from Starbucks before I headed north. The SR-520 closure made the commute feel like it was moving through syrup, so I took the long way with extra buffer and kept my head down. By the time I hit Interbay, the shipyard air smelled like metal and damp concrete, and I just stayed in my rhythm—measure, tack, weld, check, repeat. Lunch at Dough Zone in Ballard warmed me up, and later I knocked out the afternoon tasks and prepped the next weld sequence like it was already waiting for me. On the way back I stopped at QFC for brewing stuff and a couple practical home items. Tonight I met my friends at The Whale Wins, then grabbed a bagel for tomorrow in Wallingford. I’m tired, but in a good way—like the day actually lined up.
thought_process: I planned my timing around the SR-520 eastbound closure, not because I’m dramatic, but because I hate arriving to work rushed—especially when welding needs steady attention. The rain let me stay focused; it turned everything into a slower tempo, so I leaned into tasks with clear steps and measurable progress at the shipyard. I chose lunch somewhere warm and indoor—Dough Zone—because it was easier to keep my energy stable than to gamble on quick outdoor stops. The world event (bridge repair) showed up mostly as traffic stress, and I felt that early, then it faded once I got into the work. By evening, my mood warmed up with friends at The Whale Wins and the simple bagel errand felt almost comforting, like tomorrow is already handled.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
